ground_truth.title: About firmware updates for AirPods - Apple Support
ground_truth.main_content: About firmware updates for AirPods. Learn about changes and features included in the firmware updates for your AirPods. Latest AirPods firmware versions: AirPods Pro 3: 8B30, AirPods Pro 2 with MagSafe Charging Case (USB-C): 8B28, AirPods Pro 2 with MagSafe Charging Case (Lightning): 8B28, AirPods Pro 1: 6F21, AirPods 4: 8B21, AirPods 4 with Active Noise Cancellation: 8B21, AirPods 3: 6F21, AirPods 2: 6F21, AirPods 1: 6.8.8, AirPods Max (USB-C): 7E108, AirPods Max (Lightning): 6F25. Find your AirPods firmware version: You can use your iPhone, iPad, or Mac to find your AirPods firmware version. Update your AirPods firmware: Firmware updates are delivered automatically while your AirPods are charging and in Bluetooth range of your iPhone, iPad, or Mac that's connected to Wi-Fi. If your AirPods don't have the latest firmware version, you can update your firmware. Release notes detail bug fixes and feature additions including Hearing Test, Hearing Aid, Hearing Protection features, Live Translation, Siri head gestures, Adaptive Audio, Conversation Awareness, Personalized Volume, and Studio quality audio recording.
with: Firmware updates are delivered automatically while your AirPods are charging and in Bluetooth range of your iPhone, iPad, or Mac that's connected to Wi-Fi.
To use your iPhone or iPad to check that your AirPods are up to date, make sure that you have the latest version of iOS or iPadOS.
When used with an iPhone or iPad running iOS 18.1 or iPadOS 18.1 or later, AirPods Pro 2 with firmware update 7B19 enable three new features—a Hearing Test, Hearing Aid, and Hearing Protection.
Version 8A356 release notes: Firmware update 8A356 adds features and capabilities to support the new AirPods Pro 3, including heart rate sensing during workouts in the Fitness app.
